title: The keystone locks an arch by geometry alone: remove it and collapse is instant, but with it in place the structure cannot fail. Precept's rules make invalid states impossible by the shape of the definition itself — not runtime guards, but structural impossibility.
conceptualBridge: The keystone means structural impossibility because the whole system only holds when the governing rule-shape is in place.
decision: Shortlisted for a strong architectural metaphor that explains structural validity without diagramming workflows.
